items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This livestream is a practical deep dive into Amazon EBS cost optimization, covering how to identify overprovisioned volumes, right-size storage, and use the latest EBS volume types to align performance with cost. It also covers removing waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes, demonstrates monitoring tools, and provides actionable strategies to cut EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right‑size overprovisioned EBS volumes to reduce waste
Leverage newer EBS volume types to match performance needs and lower costs
Eliminate unused snapshots and outdated volumes that silently drain budgets
Use monitoring tools and real‑world examples to guide optimization
Actionable strategies aimed at cutting EBS spending without sacrificing performance
takeaways: Regular audits and right‑sizing of EBS volumes can materially reduce storage costs
Adopting appropriate, newer EBS volume types helps balance performance and spend
Cleaning up unused snapshots and obsolete volumes removes recurring, avoidable charges
Implementing monitoring and repeatable optimization practices enables ongoing cost control

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

Links provided to Team USA website and social channels (Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, TikTok).

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description… In 2015 full-time contractor Chris Broomfield purchased a five-acre parcel (reported as $27K) in upstate New York and spent the next three years commuting from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin and additional units himself. By September 2025 his property portfolio of luxury cabin rentals, commanding roughly $700/night, had generated over $2.1 million in revenue.
bullets: Purchased five-acre property in 2015 (reported $27K).
Built an A-frame cabin and additional cabins over three years while commuting from Connecticut.
Operates luxury short-term rentals listed around $700/night.
As of September 2025, the property portfolio generated over $2.1 million in revenue.
Development was owner-driven and incremental, expanding from a single cabin to multiple rentals.
takeaways: A relatively small land investment developed incrementally can produce substantial aggregated revenue over a multi-year horizon.
Staged, owner-led construction enabled expansion from one unit to multiple high‑rate rentals without immediate large-scale buildout.
High nightly pricing ($700/night) drives per-unit revenue, contributing to significant total property earnings.
The timeline from purchase (2015) to multi-million revenue (by 2025) underscores a long-term development-to-revenue cycle.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The KPMG ON AIR episode explains how operational changes—such as moving production, distribution, teams or contracts—should be assessed from transfer pricing and valuation perspectives, and why timely documentation matters. Experts Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski also outline reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), common valuation mistakes, and provide a practical checklist for companies.
bullets: Defines restructuring in the context of transfer pricing and how to identify material changes (including EBIT analysis).
Stresses the importance of preparing restructuring documentation in advance to avoid future stress and disputes.
Covers reporting duties triggered by restructurings, specifically Transfer Pricing Reporting (TPR) and Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR).
Examines the role of valuations, frequent errors, and real-life examples: moving production/distribution, employee transfers, and contract transfers.
Ends with a concise checklist of actions for firms considering operational changes.
takeaways: Assess transfer pricing and valuation impacts early when relocating or reorganizing data center or grid operations to quantify EBIT effects.
Prepare and retain restructuring documentation on time to reduce audit risk and future disputes.
Verify whether changes trigger reporting obligations (TPR, MDR) for cross-border or material operational shifts.
Treat employee and contract transfers as potentially profile-changing events that affect tax and transfer-pricing treatment.
Use a structured checklist (as discussed) to coordinate valuations, documentation and reporting before executing changes.
